findings.title: Syllable Timing Representation is Not Viable
findings.summary: The use of 'left: X%' styling for syllable placement is a static illustration, not a functional or scalable system. It does not map to the musical beat data, prevents a feasible drag-and-drop timing interaction, and will break with varying phrase lengths or time signatures.
findings.recommendation: Replace the abstract percentage-based layout with a literal, grid-based 'piano roll' or timeline view. Each beat/sub-beat position should be a discrete column or snap-point, allowing syllables to be visually and interactively mapped to their precise beat data.

findings.title: Dynamics Annotation Relies Solely on Color
findings.summary: The color-coded backgrounds for dynamics (pp, p, mf, f, etc.) are not accessible to users with color vision deficiencies (WCAG 1.4.1). While text labels exist, the color is the primary visual indicator.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a non-color-based visual signifier for dynamics. For example, vary the font-size or font-weight of the text label ('mp', 'p') itself, or use a distinct icon in addition to the color.

findings.title: Missing Workflow for Text Editing
findings.summary: The design provides no clear affordance for editing the text of a phrase or its constituent syllables. It's unclear how a user would correct a typo, change a lyric, or re-syllabify a word.
findings.recommendation: Define an interaction for entering a text-editing mode. This could be a double-click on a phrase or syllable that turns the text label into a text input field. The relationship between editing the phrase text and its impact on the syllables needs to be considered.

findings.title: Purpose of Syllable 'Selected' State is Undefined
findings.summary: A '.selected' state is styled for syllables, but the trigger and purpose are unclear. It's not specified what action causes selection or what capabilities are enabled by it (e.g., context menu, multi-select).
findings.recommendation: Clarify the selection model. For R2, define what happens on single-click vs. double-click. For example, single-click could select for a properties panel, while double-click could initiate text editing.

findings.title: Incomplete Data in 'Emotion' Dropdown
findings.summary: The mockup's 'Emotion' dropdown shows a partial list of options, missing 'neutral', 'trust', and 'fear' from the specification.
findings.recommendation: Ensure all future mockups use the complete and accurate data sets defined in the project brief to create a more realistic prototype.
